title: Create a Module Federation provider
description: Scaffold a React Module Federation provider that exposes a federated component. The bundler is selected at generation time; the generated project has no Nx-wrapped bundler config.

directory.description: Directory the provider is generated in, e.g. `apps/my-provider`. The project name (also used as the federation `name`, with hyphens converted to underscores) is inferred from the trailing path segment.
directory.x-prompt: Which directory do you want to create the provider in?

bundler.description: Bundler used by the provider. The choice is final - the bundler config is too different to switch later.

x-prompt.message: Which bundler should the provider use?

port.description: Dev-server port the provider listens on. Defaults to a non-5000 port to avoid the macOS AirTunes collision on Vite.

exposeName.description: Name under which the federated component is exposed (consumers reference `<name>/<exposeName>`).

consumer.description: Name of a consumer project whose `serve` target this provider should depend on. When set, `nx serve <provider>` also spins up the consumer (the 'serve a remote, host comes along' UX from the deprecated host/remote generators). Set automatically when the provider is generated as part of `@nx/react:consumer --providerNames=...`.
